title: i18n framework: catalogs, safe fallback, RTL, bundled offline fonts, completeness check
context: wash-guides targets multilingual distribution, prioritizing low-resource languages where WASH need is greatest. The i18n framework must handle locale negotiation with a safe fallback (never blank or broken safety text), RTL layout for languages that need it, bundled offline fonts with required glyph coverage (no runtime web-font fetch), and a build-time completeness check. This unblocks all localization tasks and must be compatible with the offline-first renderer (render-007).
objective: Implement an i18n framework for wash-guides: externalize strings and content units into locale catalogs, implement locale negotiation with safe fallback to a verified language, add RTL layout support, bundle required offline fonts with glyph coverage for target scripts, and add a build-time completeness check that fails if any locale is missing required strings or safety content.
acceptanceCriteria: Strings and content units are externalized into locale catalogs; adding a new locale requires only adding catalog files, not code changes
Locale negotiation falls back safely to a verified baseline language (never renders blank, broken, or partially-translated safety text); fallback chain is documented and tested
RTL layout is supported and exercised in the test suite; at least one test verifies correct RTL rendering for a right-to-left locale
Required fonts for all target scripts are bundled with the build output (no runtime web-font fetch); glyph coverage for target locales is verified at build time
Build-time completeness check fails if any locale catalog is missing required strings or safety-critical fields (e.g., disclaimer, danger-signs); check is integrated into pnpm build or pnpm test
TypeScript/ESM; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint all pass; reviewed and approved by a maintainer and an accessibility reviewer distinct from the author
output: A PR implementing the i18n framework with locale catalogs, safe fallback, RTL support, bundled offline fonts, and a build-time completeness check.
